task: Build the FrankX v0 Template Intelligence catalog, Visual Foundry, secure preview boundary, and multimodal creation architecture.
audience: Technical founders, creative operators, and product teams evaluating premium generative web systems.
first_read: A governed v0-to-production system with a working multimodal creation prototype.

references.label: Figma Weave capability research
references.reason: Capability research for node-based multimodal workflows; no trade dress, media, copy, or implementation copied

direction.signature: A precise node-based studio paired with a source-classified catalog and secure preview boundary.

asset_quality.why_premium: Each still demonstrates a real workflow output and remains inspectable in the product surface.

artifacts.notes: Inspected at 1440x900 and 390x844 through the six-route browser QA run.
Inspected across desktop and mobile run, review, resume, completion, dialog focus, and reduced-motion states.
Five generated outputs and their responsive in-product crops were inspected individually.
The final local run at C:/tmp/frankx-v0-qa-final/report.json passed all route, viewport, workflow, accessibility, focus, reduced-motion, and performance assertions.

checks.name: asset-tier
checks.evidence: Tier B generated proof media is appropriate for the first-party studio outputs.

checks.name: source-classification
checks.evidence: First-party, community gallery, and Vercel marketplace sources are explicitly separated.

checks.name: export-inspection
checks.evidence: Nine final WebP captures were inspected, including all six route and viewport combinations plus review and completed workflow states.

checks.name: visual-qa
checks.evidence: Six route and viewport combinations passed with no overflow, clipping, duplicate IDs, unnamed controls, broken visible images, console errors, or local request failures.

checks.name: accessibility
checks.evidence: Desktop dialog and mobile inspector focus traps, Escape behavior, trigger restoration, accessible names, alt text, and reduced-motion behavior passed.

checks.name: performance
checks.evidence: Local FCP, resource-count, and decoded-script budgets passed on every tested route and viewport.

checks.name: engineering-gates
checks.evidence: Twenty-two behavior tests, TypeScript, scoped ESLint, and the Next.js production build passed.

checks.name: secure-preview
checks.evidence: Behavior tests cover cache hit and miss, refresh invalidation, nested assets, method rejection, safe fallback, and request and response credential stripping.

checks.name: independent-review
checks.evidence: External v0 Max review is intentionally deferred until the draft PR and Vercel preview are available.

checks.name: production-artifact
checks.evidence: The Vercel preview has not been created; production promotion remains human-gated.

score.notes: Local design and engineering evidence clears the 26/30 premium gate; external preview review remains before the ship decision.

known_risks: v0 Platform API v2 and the exact SDK package are beta.
Generated identity studies are concept material, not final vector identity.
Live image, video, and 3D providers are not connected.
Repo-wide security scan reports pre-existing findings outside this lane.
